aboutFounder.name: Viktor Zlatovcen
aboutFounder.lead: I see design not as a collection of visual decisions, but as precise work with space, where every detail has meaning and purpose. My projects are built on thoughtful planning, considered ergonomics, and the desire to create interiors that are not only aesthetic, but genuinely comfortable to live in.
aboutFounder.text: As the founder of Zlatovcen Art Design, I pay close attention to the balance between architectural logic and visual expression. For me, it is essential that a space feels complete, contemporary, and relevant over time.
aboutFounder.imageAlt: Viktor Zlatovcen, founder of Zlatovcen Art Design

testimonialShowcase.quote: Every detail felt considered, refined, and impeccably resolved from start to finish.
testimonialShowcase.note: Working with Zlatovcen Art Design was smooth and comfortable throughout the entire process.

items.title: Full design project
items.description: We develop more than a beautiful interior — we create a considered space where every detail, from planning to lighting, works for comfort, aesthetics, and ease of living. Special attention is given to smart planning, ergonomics, and movement logic, so the interior is not only visually refined, but also truly comfortable in everyday life. The project includes a full cycle of work, from idea and concept to realization.
includes: Selection of materials, furniture, lighting, sanitaryware, and more

items.title: Design project without visualization
items.description: A complete planning and technical interior solution without the 3D visualization stage. This format is suitable for those who want to move quickly into renovation with a clear understanding of the layout, furniture placement, and all technical decisions. We develop the space with standards, ergonomics, and movement logic in mind, and provide a full set of drawings and implementation recommendations so you can begin renovation confidently, without unnecessary mistakes or rework.
includes: Assistance with selecting materials, furniture, lighting, sanitaryware, and more

items.title: Architectural design
items.description: Development of the architectural appearance of a building with attention to proportions, materials, and overall style. We create a modern and harmonious facade that emphasizes the architecture of the home and makes it feel visually complete and aesthetically expressive. All key elements are carefully developed: finishing materials, color solutions, lighting, and facade details, so the final result feels considered and realistic to implement.
includes: Development of the facade concept and stylistic direction
Selection of materials and color solutions
Development of architectural details and elements

items.question: What services do you provide?
items.answer: We offer residential and commercial interior design, including space planning, furniture selection, and bespoke design solutions. Our services also include project management and design for renovations.

items.question: How do I start a project with your studio?
items.answer: We begin with a personal consultation to understand your vision, lifestyle, and spatial needs. After that, we develop a tailored design direction, timeline, and project framework.

items.question: Do you work on full home transformations?
items.answer: Yes, we design both complete residential spaces and individual rooms. Every project receives the same level of attention to detail, coherence, and sensitivity to materials.

groups.imageAlt: Luxury living room with a sculptural furniture composition

items.question: Can you work on a project remotely?
items.answer: Yes, we can collaborate remotely depending on the scale and location of the project. We structure the process carefully to preserve clarity, visual precision, and a seamless client experience.

items.question: Do you help with furniture and material selection?
items.answer: Absolutely. We select furniture, finishes, lighting, and decor so that every element supports the overall atmosphere and identity of the interior.

items.question: How long does an interior design project usually take?
items.answer: The timeline depends on the scale and complexity of the project. After the first consultation, we provide a clear process outline with approximate stages and implementation timelines.

interior-design-price-chisinau-moldova.title: How Much Does Interior Design Cost in Moldova
interior-design-price-chisinau-moldova.metaTitle: How Much Does Interior Design Cost in Chișinău and Moldova | ZLATOVCEN ART DESIGN
interior-design-price-chisinau-moldova.metaDescription: We explain how much interior design costs in Chișinău and Moldova, what an interior design project includes, what affects the price per square meter, and how to choose the right working format.
interior-design-price-chisinau-moldova.excerpt: The cost of an interior design project depends not only on the size of the space, but also on the depth of work: planning, visualizations, technical drawings, material selection, and implementation support.
interior-design-price-chisinau-moldova.previewImageAlt: Premium living room interior for an article about the cost of an interior design project in Chișinău and Moldova
interior-design-price-chisinau-moldova.featuredImageAlt: Elegant interior with premium furniture and a carefully considered layout
interior-design-price-chisinau-moldova.heroImageAlt: Luxury interior for an article about interior design pricing in Moldova
interior-design-price-chisinau-moldova.backgroundImageAlt: Dark interior atmosphere for an article about the cost of an interior design project
interior-design-price-chisinau-moldova.inlineImageAlt: Premium interior composition with soft light and refined materials
interior-design-price-chisinau-moldova.quote: A good design project saves not only time, but also budget, because many mistakes are resolved before renovation begins.

sections.heading: What affects the cost of an interior design project
content: The price of interior design in Chișinău and Moldova is not calculated only by the size of the space. The final cost is influenced by the type of property, the complexity of the layout, the number of rooms, the level of detail, the need for 3D visualization, material selection, project furnishing, and implementation support.

The scope of work will differ for an apartment, a house, a commercial space, or an architectural facade. Sometimes a client only needs a well-planned layout and technical drawings, while in other cases a full interior design project is required — with visualization, selection of furniture, lighting, sanitaryware, materials, and implementation control.

sections.heading: Why the price per square meter can vary
content: Two spaces with the same area can have completely different levels of complexity. For example, an 80 m² apartment with a simple layout and a clear stylistic direction requires less time than an 80 m² house with complex architecture, replanning, custom furniture, and detailed engineering solutions.

Professional interior design is not just a beautiful image. It is work with spatial logic, ergonomics, circulation routes, lighting, storage, materials, technical details, and the real possibility of implementing the project without chaos on site.

sections.heading: What interior design project formats are available
content: Clients usually have several working formats to choose from. A full design project includes planning, 3D visualizations, working drawings, selection of materials, furniture, lighting, sanitaryware, and support through implementation. This format is suitable for those who want a fully considered solution and want to avoid random decisions during renovation.

A design project without visualization is suitable for clients who primarily need layout planning, furniture placement, technical solutions, and drawings. It is a faster and more accessible format, but it requires more involvement from the client in the visual part.

sections.heading: Why an interior design project helps save money
content: At first glance, an interior design project may seem like an additional expense. In practice, it often helps avoid rework, incorrect purchases, uncomfortable layouts, poor lighting, and mistakes with sockets, plumbing, doors, furniture, and finishes.

When all decisions are made in advance, renovation becomes easier to manage. Builders receive clear drawings, the client understands the final result, and the designer helps preserve a unified aesthetic and functionality.

sections.heading: How to understand what budget you need
content: If you are planning to renovate an apartment, house, or commercial space in Chișinău, Comrat, or another city in Moldova, it is better to start not with buying materials, but with a consultation and an assessment of the project goals. At this stage, it becomes clear which format is truly needed: a full design project, a project without visualization, or an architectural concept.

ZLATOVCEN ART DESIGN creates interiors where aesthetics are combined with logic, comfort, and real implementation. This approach is especially important for premium spaces, where every detail should look intentional, precise, and professional.

paragraphs: The cost of an interior design project depends not only on the size of the space, but also on the depth of work: planning, visualizations, technical drawings, material selection, and implementation support.

The price of interior design in Chișinău and Moldova is not calculated only by the size of the space. The final cost is influenced by the type of property, the complexity of the layout, the number of rooms, the level of detail, the need for 3D visualization, material selection, project furnishing, and implementation support.

Two spaces with the same area can have completely different levels of complexity. For example, an 80 m² apartment with a simple layout and a clear stylistic direction requires less time than an 80 m² house with complex architecture, replanning, custom furniture, and detailed engineering solutions.

Professional interior design is not just a beautiful image. It is work with spatial logic, ergonomics, circulation routes, lighting, storage, materials, technical details, and the real possibility of implementing the project without chaos on site.

If you are planning to renovate an apartment, house, or commercial space in Chișinău, Comrat, or another city in Moldova, it is better to start not with buying materials, but with a consultation and an assessment of the project goals.

what-is-included-in-interior-design-proj.title: What Is Included in an Interior Design Project
what-is-included-in-interior-design-proj.metaTitle: What Is Included in an Interior Design Project | Stages, Drawings and Visualization
what-is-included-in-interior-design-proj.metaDescription: We explain in detail what an interior design project includes: planning, zoning, 3D visualization, working drawings, material selection, and renovation support.
what-is-included-in-interior-design-proj.excerpt: An interior design project is not a set of beautiful images, but a detailed system of decisions that allows an interior to be implemented properly, without chaos or unnecessary rework.
what-is-included-in-interior-design-proj.previewImageAlt: Bright premium interior for an article about what an interior design project includes

what-is-included-in-interior-design-proj.inlineImageAlt: Detailed interior scene with furniture, light, and materials
what-is-included-in-interior-design-proj.quote: A strong design project answers not only the question “how will it look beautiful,” but also “how can it be implemented.”
keywords: what is included in a design project

sections.heading: Space planning and zoning
content: The first and one of the most important stages of an interior design project is planning. This is where the logic of the future interior is defined: where the kitchen, lounge area, storage, bedroom, workspace, bathroom, circulation routes, and main usage scenarios will be located.

A well-planned layout makes an interior comfortable in everyday life. Even the most expensive renovation will not feel premium if the space is inconvenient to move through, lacks storage, or furniture blocks natural circulation.

sections.heading: Concept and visual style
content: After the planning solution is developed, the visual concept is formed. This is not simply the selection of beautiful references, but the creation of a unified image of the interior: color palette, materials, mood, level of contrast, furniture shapes, lighting character, and decorative accents.

For a premium interior studio, it is especially important that the space feels complete. In a strong project there are no random elements: doors, walls, furniture, lighting, textiles, and decor all work within one aesthetic system.

sections.heading: 3D visualization of the future interior
content: 3D visualization helps you see in advance how the interior will look after implementation. This is especially important for clients who find it difficult to imagine a space only from plans and technical drawings.

Visualization allows materials, colors, furniture, lighting, and atmosphere to be approved before renovation begins. This reduces the risk of mistakes and helps decisions be made faster and with greater confidence.

sections.heading: Working drawings for renovation
content: Working drawings are the technical foundation of an interior design project. They include demolition and construction plans, furniture layout, lighting plans, sockets, switches, plumbing, ceilings, floors, wall elevations, and other documents required for implementation.

Without drawings, renovation often turns into a constant flow of questions on site. With drawings, builders understand what needs to be done, the client can control the process, and the designer preserves the quality of the project.

sections.heading: Selection of materials, furniture, and lighting
content: A high-quality design project includes the selection of finishing materials, furniture, lighting, sanitaryware, and decorative elements. This helps maintain a unified style and prevents expensive elements from looking disconnected.

In a premium interior, it is not only individual pieces that matter, but also the way they work together: the texture of wood, the shade of stone, the temperature of light, metal, fabric, furniture shape, and proportions.

sections.heading: Implementation support
content: Even a perfectly prepared project requires attention during implementation. During renovation, technical questions, material substitutions, dimension clarifications, and details may appear that need to be resolved without losing the overall concept.
Implementation support helps preserve the author’s concept and bring the interior to a result that matches the project, rather than becoming a set of compromises.

paragraphs: An interior design project is not a set of beautiful images, but a detailed system of decisions that allows an interior to be implemented properly, without chaos or unnecessary rework.

The first and one of the most important stages of an interior design project is planning. This is where the logic of the future interior is defined: where the kitchen, lounge area, storage, bedroom, workspace, bathroom, circulation routes, and main usage scenarios will be located.

After the planning solution is developed, the visual concept is formed. This is not simply the selection of beautiful references, but the creation of a unified image of the interior: color palette, materials, mood, level of contrast, furniture shapes, lighting character, and decorative accents.

3D visualization helps you see in advance how the interior will look after implementation. This is especially important for clients who find it difficult to imagine a space only from plans and technical drawings.

A high-quality design project includes the selection of finishing materials, furniture, lighting, sanitaryware, and decorative elements. This helps maintain a unified style and prevents expensive elements from looking disconnected.

renovation-without-designer-mistakes.title: Designerless Renovation Failures
renovation-without-designer-mistakes.metaTitle: Renovation Mistakes Without an Interior Designer | How to Avoid Rework
renovation-without-designer-mistakes.metaDescription: We explain the main renovation mistakes made without a designer: inconvenient planning, wrong lighting, sockets, furniture, materials, and lack of working drawings.
renovation-without-designer-mistakes.excerpt: The most expensive renovation mistakes often happen not because of poor materials, but because there is no thoughtful planning, no working drawings, and no clear interior logic.
renovation-without-designer-mistakes.previewImageAlt: Interior detail for an article about renovation mistakes without a designer
renovation-without-designer-mistakes.featuredImageAlt: Premium living room with a carefully considered composition and lighting
renovation-without-designer-mistakes.heroImageAlt: Interior for an article about renovation mistakes without a design project
renovation-without-designer-mistakes.backgroundImageAlt: Dark interior background for an article about renovation mistakes
renovation-without-designer-mistakes.inlineImageAlt: Stylish living room with furniture, light, and decorative details
renovation-without-designer-mistakes.quote: Rework is almost always more expensive than the right decision made at the project stage.

sections.heading: Mistake 1. Starting renovation without a plan
content: One of the most common mistakes is starting renovation before the layout has been properly thought through. At this stage, it may seem enough to choose beautiful materials and find builders, but it is the layout that determines the comfort of the future interior.

If daily scenarios, furniture, storage, circulation routes, and zones are not defined in advance, the result may be a beautiful renovation that is uncomfortable to live in. This leads to unnecessary purchases, compromises, and rework.

sections.heading: Mistake 2. Planning electrical points before choosing furniture
content: Sockets, switches, and lighting points should be designed after the furniture layout and usage scenarios are understood. If electrical planning is done too early, sockets may end up behind a wardrobe, or not where a desk, bedside table, or kitchen appliance actually needs them.

A professional interior design project connects furniture, lighting, and electrical planning into one system. This is especially important for kitchens, bedrooms, living rooms, wardrobes, and bathrooms.

sections.heading: Mistake 3. Using only one light source
content: Flat overhead light makes an interior feel less layered and less comfortable. Modern interior design requires several lighting levels: general lighting, accent lighting, decorative lighting, integrated lighting, and local lighting scenarios.
Light is what creates atmosphere in the evening, emphasizes materials, highlights architectural lines, and makes the space feel visually more refined.

sections.heading: Mistake 4. Buying materials without an overall concept
content: Even expensive materials can look random if they are selected separately from one another. Tile, wood, stone, paint, metal, textiles, and furniture should work within one palette and one logic.
Without a design concept, it is easy to end up with an interior where every element may look beautiful on its own, but together they do not create a sense of unity.

sections.heading: Mistake 5. Saving on working drawings
content: Working drawings are not a formality; they are a tool for controlling renovation. They help builders understand the decisions, and they help the client see exactly what needs to be implemented.
When there are no drawings, many decisions are made directly on site. This almost always leads to haste, mistakes, and a result that differs from expectations.

sections.heading: How to avoid renovation mistakes
content: The best way to avoid mistakes is to start with an interior design project. It allows you to see the future interior in advance and coordinate the layout, materials, lighting, furniture, and technical decisions.
For apartments, houses, and commercial spaces in Chișinău and Moldova, this is especially important: a well-developed project makes renovation clear, manageable, and visually coherent.

paragraphs: The most expensive renovation mistakes often happen not because of poor materials, but because there is no thoughtful planning, no working drawings, and no clear interior logic.

One of the most common mistakes is starting renovation before the layout has been properly thought through. At this stage, it may seem enough to choose beautiful materials and find builders, but it is the layout that determines the comfort of the future interior.

Sockets, switches, and lighting points should be designed after the furniture layout and usage scenarios are understood. If electrical planning is done too early, sockets may end up behind a wardrobe, or not where a desk, bedside table, or kitchen appliance actually needs them.

Flat overhead light makes an interior feel less layered and less comfortable. Modern interior design requires several lighting levels: general lighting, accent lighting, decorative lighting, integrated lighting, and local lighting scenarios.
Even expensive materials can look random if they are selected separately from one another. Tile, wood, stone, paint, metal, textiles, and furniture should work within one palette and one logic.

The best way to avoid mistakes is to start with an interior design project. It allows you to see the future interior in advance and coordinate the layout, materials, lighting, furniture, and technical decisions.

how-to-choose-interior-designer-moldova.excerpt: A good interior designer is not just someone with taste, but a specialist who knows how to bring together aesthetics, planning, technical decisions, and implementation.

how-to-choose-interior-designer-moldova.quote: Choose not just a style, but an approach: a good interior must be beautiful, comfortable, and possible to implement.

sections.heading: Look beyond beautiful images and study the logic of projects
content: When choosing an interior designer in Moldova, it is important to look not only at the visual appeal of a portfolio. A good project should show thoughtful planning, proper zoning, comfortable movement, work with lighting, storage, and materials.

A beautiful render may make an impression, but professional design is revealed in how comfortable the interior is in real life and how realistically it can be implemented without constant compromises.

sections.heading: Evaluate style and visual culture
content: Every studio has its own signature. For a premium interior, the style should feel mature rather than template-based: a restrained palette, correct proportions, quality materials, soft light, clean geometry, and a sense of completeness.

If you want an interior in the style of luxury minimalism, quiet luxury, or contemporary classic, choose a designer who knows how to work with subtle shades, textures, and atmosphere — not only with bright decorative effects.

sections.heading: Clarify what is included in the design project
content: Before starting work, it is important to understand exactly what the service includes. A full interior design project usually includes planning solutions, 3D visualization, working drawings, selection of materials, furniture, lighting, sanitaryware, and implementation consultations.
If the price seems too low, it is worth checking whether important stages are missing: drawings, visualization, material selection, or project support.

sections.heading: Check how the designer works with implementation
content: An interior does not end with a beautiful presentation. It is important that the project can be implemented in reality: with clear dimensions, materials, technical decisions, and logic for builders.

A good designer understands the construction process, communication with contractors, possible material substitutions, and the limitations of the property. This is especially important for houses, apartments, and commercial spaces, where implementation mistakes can be costly.

sections.heading: Discuss the budget and expectations in advance
content: A professional designer should not promise a premium interior without understanding the budget. The cost of renovation, furniture, lighting, materials, and work should be discussed in advance so the project matches the client’s real possibilities.
An honest conversation about budget helps avoid a situation where the visualization looks expensive, but implementation becomes unrealistic.

sections.heading: Why local experience matters
content: If you are looking for an interior designer in Chișinău, Comrat, Gagauzia, or another region of Moldova, local experience can be an advantage. The designer better understands material availability, delivery specifics, contractors, timelines, and the realities of the local market.
ZLATOVCEN ART DESIGN works with interiors where not only aesthetics and status matter, but also the real possibility of implementing the project.

paragraphs: A good interior designer is not just someone with taste, but a specialist who knows how to bring together aesthetics, planning, technical decisions, and implementation.

When choosing an interior designer in Moldova, it is important to look not only at the visual appeal of a portfolio. A good project should show thoughtful planning, proper zoning, comfortable movement, work with lighting, storage, and materials.

Every studio has its own signature. For a premium interior, the style should feel mature rather than template-based: a restrained palette, correct proportions, quality materials, soft light, clean geometry, and a sense of completeness.

Before starting work, it is important to understand exactly what the service includes. A full interior design project usually includes planning solutions, 3D visualization, working drawings, selection of materials, furniture, lighting, sanitaryware, and implementation consultations.

If you are looking for an interior designer in Chișinău, Comrat, Gagauzia, or another region of Moldova, local experience can be an advantage. The designer better understands material availability, delivery specifics, contractors, timelines, and the realities of the local market.
